title: User Authentication
description: Customer registration, login, and logout flows handled by servlet controllers delegating to UserServiceImpl

actors.name: Customer
actors.description: Registered end-user who can register, login, and logout

actors.name: Admin
actors.description: Hardcoded admin user (admin@gmail.com / admin) with direct session bypass — no service layer

use_cases.name: Register
use_cases.entry_point: GET/POST with params: username, mobile, email, address, pincode, password, confirmPassword
steps: Construct UserBean(userName, mobileNo, emailId, address, pinCode, password)
Check isRegistered(emailId) — if true return 'Email Id Already Registered!'
INSERT INTO user table via PreparedStatement
On success: send email via MailMessage.registrationSuccess(email, firstName)
outcomes: Duplicate email: 'Email Id Already Registered!' → register.jsp

use_cases.name: Login
use_cases.entry_point: GET/POST with params: username, password, usertype (admin|customer)
steps: If admin: hardcoded check (admin@gmail.com / admin), set session attrs, forward to adminViewProduct.jsp
If valid: call UserServiceImpl.getUserDetails(email, password) → UserBean
Set session attrs: userdata(UserBean), username, password, usertype
outcomes: Customer success → userHome.jsp (session populated with UserBean)
Failure → login.jsp?message=Login Denied! Invalid Username or password.

use_cases.name: Logout
steps: Nullify session attrs: username, password, usertype, userdata

extend_relationships: Login <<extend>> Admin Login (hardcoded credential check, bypasses UserService)

key_classes.name: UserServiceImpl
methods: registerUser(String userName, Long mobileNo, String emailId, String address, int pinCode, String password): String

database.access: JDBC via DBUtil.provideConnection(), plain-text password storage

important_notes: Passwords stored and compared in plain-text — no hashing
Admin credentials are hardcoded in LoginSrv (admin@gmail.com / admin), no DB lookup
MailMessage.registrationSuccess() sends welcome email on successful registration
Both doGet and doPost delegate to same handler (doPost calls doGet)
UserServiceImpl is instantiated directly (new UserServiceImpl()) — no DI framework
